name_en: Materials Relating to Sakuma Yoken's Japanese Mathematics (Wasan)
description_en: A tangible cultural property consisting of materials relating to the Japanese mathematics (wasan) work of Sakuma Yoken. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2008-11-25. Located at Funehiki-machi Ishimori, Toya.

name_romaji: Iwasaki-ken Kankatsu Dai-yon Daiku Shōshiku Tamura-gun Ashizawa-mura Jibiki Shukuzu
name_en: Cadastral Reduced Map of Ashizawa Village, Tamura District, Iwasaki Prefecture
description_en: A tangible cultural property consisting of a reduced cadastral map of Ashizawa Village, Tamura District, in the Fourth Greater District, Lesser Fourth District of the former Iwasaki Prefecture administration. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2008-11-25. Located at Funehiki-machi Funehiki, Minami-motomachi.
A tangible cultural property consisting of a double-mouthed earthenware vessel. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2008-11-25. Located at Funehiki-machi Funehiki, Minami-motomachi.

A tangible cultural property consisting of a small gilt-bronze Buddhist image known as the armored buddha (yoroibutsu). Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2008-11-25. Located at Funehiki-machi Funehiki, Minami-motomachi.

name_en: Pink Weeping Cherry of Daishoji Temple
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the pink weeping cherry (benishidare) at Daishoji Temple. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2010-04-06. Located at Funehiki-machi Sasayama, Tateishi.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of the Hatanaka grand kagura (daikagura). Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2011-02-23. Located at Takine-machi Sugaya, Hatanaka.

name_en: Jizo Cherry Tree of Zesaiji Temple
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the Jizo cherry tree at Zesaiji Temple. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2013-02-13. Located at Funehiki-machi Ashizawa, Zesaiji-mae.
A natural monument consisting of the great ginkgo tree at Jokoji Temple. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2013-04-04. Located at Tokiwa-machi Tokiwa, Nishida.
A natural monument consisting of the weeping cherry (shidarezakura) at Shogakuji Temple. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2014-06-03. Located at Funehiki-machi Minami-utsushi, Machi.
A tangible cultural property consisting of materials relating to Ito Naoki. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2015-03-16. Located at Funehiki-machi Arawada, Kurouchi.

name_en: Reduced Map of Kadosawa Village (Materials Relating to Sakuma Yoken)
description_en: A tangible cultural property consisting of a reduced map of Kadosawa Village, part of the materials relating to Sakuma Yoken. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2023-11-22. Located at Funehiki-machi Kadosawa, Doyama.
A historic site comprising thirty-three Kannon images at Ashizawa Hongo. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2024-11-26. Located at Funehiki-machi Ashizawa, Tate.
A natural monument consisting of a cherry tree known as the Cherry Tree of Hope. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 1979-05-30. Located at Sakasagawa.
A tangible cultural property (building) consisting of the main gate (sanmon) of Renkeji Temple. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 1980-05-31. Located at Shinmachi.
A natural monument consisting of the Japanese umbrella pine (koyamaki) at Hanazono. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 1981-12-18. Located at Hanazono.

name_en: Enshrinement Transfer Rite of Omasu Myojin
description_en: An intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the enshrinement transfer (senza) rite of Omasu Myojin. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 1986-10-20. Located at Fukui, Tamano, Isshiki, and Minowa.
A tangible cultural property (building) consisting of the tea room associated with Abe Masakata. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 2006-03-24. Located at Tanagura.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated bronze statue of the eleven-headed Kannon Bodhisattva. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 2007-04-02. Located at Yatsuki.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing bronze statue of the Kannon Bodhisattva. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 2007-04-02. Located at Yatsuki.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing bronze statue of the Jizo Bodhisattva. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 2007-04-02. Located at Furumachi.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing wooden statue of Daikokuten. Town-designated by Tanagura Town on 2007-04-02. Located at Baba.
A cultural property consisting of a seated statue of Yakushi Ruriko Nyorai, the Medicine Buddha. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village.

name_en: Main Hall of Onsen Hachiman Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property (building) consisting of the main hall of Onsen Hachiman Shrine. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Takatera 56-1, Yumoto, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A tangible cultural property (building) consisting of the main hall of Takekuma Shrine. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Miyashita, Osato, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A tangible cultural property (building) consisting of the main hall of Toyokashima Shrine. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Miyanomae 49, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.
A historic site known as Chigobashi. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Chigobashi 54-3, Shimo-matsumoto, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.
A historic site consisting of the ruins of Ushigajo Castle. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Mukaidate, Osato, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A historic (archaeological) site known as Itagoya. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located in the Kamabusayama national forest, Tarao, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A historic (archaeological) site known as Futatsuki-no-Matsu. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Futatsukimatsu 146, Osato, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.
A historic site consisting of the ruins of Tsumurodate Castle. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Kasugayama, Iide, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.
A historic site consisting of Itamiya Shrine. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Nakayashiki 53, Shiroko, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.
A historic site consisting of Onabe Shrine. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located within the national forest at Yumoto, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A historic site consisting of the cliff-carved buddha (magaibutsu) of Haguroyama. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Haguroyama 3, Osato, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A historic site consisting of a group of stone stupas (itabi) at Yokouchi. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Ikegamiyama 27, Shimo-matsumoto, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

name_en: Great Cypress of Seiryuji Kannon Hall
description_en: A natural monument (plant) consisting of the great Japanese cypress (hinoki) at the Kanzeon hall of Seiryuji Temple. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Ryusei 34, Makinouchi, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

name_en: Weeping Cherry in the Grounds of Kisshoin Temple
description_en: A natural monument (plant) consisting of the weeping cherry (shidarezakura) in the grounds of Kisshoin Temple. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Nakayashiki 29, Shiroko, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A natural monument (pond/marsh) consisting of the Wakui spring water. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1982-09-01. Located at Kyoyabara 6, Makinouchi, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated statue of Dainichi Nyorai. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1987-05-15. Located at Idaira 50, Yumoto, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Shō Kannon Bosatsu Ryūzō to Zushi
name_en: Standing Wooden Sacred Kannon Bodhisattva and Its Shrine Cabinet
description_en: A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing wooden statue of the sacred Kannon Bodhisattva together with its zushi shrine cabinet. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1987-05-15. Located at Ryusei, Makinouchi, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.

name_romaji: Yakushi Nyorai Zazō oyobi Wakiji Ni-Bosatsu Ryūzō
name_en: Seated Yakushi Nyorai and Two Standing Attendant Bodhisattvas
description_en: A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated statue of Yakushi Nyorai together with two standing attendant bodhisattvas. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village on 1987-05-15. Located at Gongenmori 1, Kami-matsumoto, Ten'ei-mura, Iwase District, Fukushima Prefecture.
